caseStudies.title: Payment Conversion Optimization
caseStudies.description: Lifting checkout conversion through frictionless payment flows.
caseStudies.overview: A subscription SaaS company was losing a significant portion of potential customers at the payment step due to a cumbersome multi-page checkout flow, limited payment method support, and poor mobile UX. The goal was to redesign the payment experience to minimise drop-off.
caseStudies.problem: The checkout flow required 7 form fields and 3 page transitions, with no support for digital wallets or local payment methods. Mobile conversion rates were 40% lower than desktop, and error messages were cryptic and unhelpful.
caseStudies.solution: The checkout was rebuilt as a single-page experience with smart field autofill, real-time card validation, and support for 12 local payment methods including digital wallets. Error messages were redesigned with actionable guidance. Link-based checkout was introduced for returning customers.

results.summary: The redesigned checkout is the highest-converting in the company's history. The addition of local payment methods opened new markets in Southeast Asia and Latin America.

caseStudies.title: Subscription Revenue Recovery
caseStudies.description: Automated dunning and payment retry to reduce involuntary churn.
caseStudies.overview: A media subscription company was losing 8% of its subscriber base monthly to failed payments — most of which were recoverable card declines rather than intentional cancellations. Manual follow-up via email was ineffective and delayed.
caseStudies.problem: Failed payments were retried only once, 24 hours after the initial failure, with no smart retry logic accounting for card issuer patterns. Recovery emails were generic and sent days after the failure, when customer intent had already faded.
caseStudies.solution: An intelligent dunning system was implemented with ML-optimized retry timing based on card network patterns. Personalised recovery email sequences were triggered within minutes of payment failure, with contextual messaging based on the failure reason. In-product payment update prompts were added for mobile users.

results.summary: Involuntary churn is no longer a major revenue leak. The recovery system operates fully automatically, requiring no manual intervention, and continues to improve as it learns from payment outcomes.

caseStudies.title: Global Marketplace Payouts
caseStudies.overview: A freelance marketplace needed to scale its seller payout infrastructure from 5 to 40 supported countries, handling multi-currency disbursements, local compliance requirements, and tax reporting obligations across jurisdictions with vastly different banking systems.
caseStudies.problem: International sellers waited up to 14 days for payouts via wire transfer, with high fees eating into earnings. Currency conversion was opaque, and compliance documentation for each new country had to be implemented from scratch.
caseStudies.solution: A unified payout orchestration layer was built to route payments through the optimal rails for each country — local ACH equivalents where available, instant payment schemes where supported. Currency conversion rates were locked at transaction time with transparent fee disclosure. A compliance framework was implemented to handle local KYC and tax documentation requirements.

results.summary: Sellers in new markets report dramatically improved trust in the platform due to fast, transparent payouts. The payout infrastructure is now a competitive differentiator in seller acquisition.
